articles.title: Donald Trump’s new Hormuz deadline, ceasefire push and rescue presser: What we know on US-Iran war in its 6th week
articles.description: More than 5,000 people have been killed in the West Asia war far so far. The causalities are almost three-quarters of them in Iran, according to government organizations and the US-based Human Rights Activists News Agency.

topStories.title: Who was Majid Khademi? Iran's Revolutionary Guards Intelligence head killed in US strikes
topStories.description: Majid Khademi took over as head of the intelligence organisation of Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps in 2025 after Israeli air strikes killed his predecessor, Mohammad Khotami.

topStories.title: ‘Donald Trump is dead’ trends again: Social media continues to speculate about US President’s health
topStories.description: Rumors about Donald Trump's health have intensified due to his age and brief absences. Social media exploded with claims of a medical emergency after a routine press lid, prompting official denials and a Trump post asserting his good health.

topStories.title: Amid Trump health scare, here's the reason why US President skipped Easter Sunday events
topStories.description: Amidst speculation over Donald Trump's health due to missed appearances, the White House issued a statement. Despite rumors of hospitalization, Trump continued his rhetoric on Iran.

topStories.description: Trump is now under pressure, not just from allies but also within; the President's approval rating is the lowest it has ever been and mid-term elections are only seven months away.

dailyNews.title: Navy SEAL Team 6, CIA involved in US airman's rescue from Iran: How downed officer survived with just pistol, comms gear
dailyNews.description: When the F-15 jet was hit on Friday, both pilot and the weapons systems officer had ejected, but their fates had been different: while the pilot was found shortly after the incident, his crew member remained missing, sparking a frantic search by both US and Iran.

dailyNews.title: AI needs more power: Offices could be the answer

dailyNews.title: Angels’ Mike Trout calls out Mariners' risky strategy after getting hit by pitch
dailyNews.description: Mike Trout left Sunday's game after being hit by a pitch but X-rays were negative. The Los Angeles Angels star expressed frustration with Seattle Mariners' pitcher control.

dailyNews.title: Construction crew unearths surprising 300-year-old cannon while digging in historic city
dailyNews.description: In only the third such discovery in 30 years, according to archaeologists, construction workers in Kingston upon Hull unearthed a rare 300-year-old cast-iron cannon.

dailyNews.title: King Charles sparks backlash from UK Christians by not delivering an Easter message this year
dailyNews.description: King Charles faces backlash from UK Christians for not giving an Easter message after he previously wished Muslims a blessed Ramadan, experts say.

dailyNews.description: Sen. Warnock compares Christian leaders backing Trump to those who justified slavery, saying their faith-based support for the president is misguided.

dailyNews.description: In an expletive-laced social media post, the president said Iran should open the Strait of Hormuz or he would bomb bridges and power plants.

dailyNews.title: Plunging International Student Enrollment Under Trump Squeezes Colleges
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration’s campaign to curtail international students is not just hitting the elite schools targeted by the government.

dailyNews.title: Forget the A.I. Apocalypse. Memes Have Already Nuked Our Culture.
dailyNews.description: From our jokes and slang to the White House’s policy messaging, internet “brain rot” has escaped our phones to take over … well, everything.

dailyNews.title: CIA World Factbook shuttered by Trump administration
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration has shut down the CIA World Factbook, and there's much lamenting about the demise of a free, trusted source many people used to check basic facts about countries.

dailyNews.title: "WE GOT HIM!," Trump declares after U.S. airman rescued deep inside Iran
dailyNews.description: President Trump hailed the rescue of a U.S. airman who was missing almost two days inside Iran — and threatened to hit power plants if Iran doesn't reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: 4/5: CBS Weekend News
dailyNews.description: Latest details on daring mission to rescue U.S. airman from Iran after fighter jet shot down; Trump sends profanity-laden threat to Iran.

dailyNews.title: Missing U.S. crew member from downed fighter jet rescued in Iran, Trump says
dailyNews.description: A U.S. crew member who went missing when an F-15E fighter jet was shot down over a remote area of Iran has been rescued by U.S. forces.

dailyNews.title: Latest on Iran: U.S. airman rescued, Trump makes profanity-laced ultimatum
dailyNews.description: Charlie D'Agata and Taurean Small have the latest news on the Iran war, including details on the rescue of a U.S. airman whose fighter jet was shot down and President Trump's new demand for the Iranians to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Trump threatens Tuesday strikes on Iran's power plants and bridges in profanity-laden post
dailyNews.description: President Trump threatened to hit Iranian power plants and bridges on Tuesday as he posted another ultimatum on social media Sunday morning. "Open the F*****' Strait, you crazy bastards, or you'll be living in Hell," the president wrote. Taurean Small reports
